entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 14 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 14 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-14 19:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • $7.50 for a toaster or $91.90 for a bucket of paint? The price is wrong
  • A Wallaby threw his World Cup medal into Sydney Harbour. He regretted it ever since
  • Stuck in the middle: Saints, Dogs at the crossroads
  • Italian government on brink of collapse as PM Draghi offers resignation
  • Here’s why we must build the Suburban Rail Loop now
  • Informal support at universities is key to students’ wellbeing
  • This spectacular Sorrento property is one of Australia’s best private gardens
  • Why this Spanish energy tycoon is Australia’s fortune teller
  • Senior public servant linked to council CEO’s yacht fundraising
  • Wellness apps and fitness trackers: Why insurers love your smartwatch
  • The best flowers to pick in winter – and how to make them last
  • The Barilaro case has precedents - and only the ICAC can deliver the truth
  • Home quirky home: Instagram accounts celebrate Sydney suburbia
  • Labor confident health will remain a strong suit
  • Australia’s current approach to COVID embodies a simple contradiction
  • Meet the ASX chief executive who receives 5% of her salary in Bitcoin
  • Problem gaming leading to aggression, school refusal and self-harm
  • Labor’s plan to cut carbon pollution faces major integrity test
  • ‘Heart of Nedlands’ triple-tower project up for decision again – now with giant waterfall!
  • DJ Albo has vinyl word on album diplomacy
  • ‘No time for politics’: Housing ministers to meet to tackle affordability and homelessness
  • Scott Morrison accuses China of supporting Putin’s war crimes

  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch

  • Best of cartoons, July 15, 2022

  • Plan to end family violence must reflect Indigenous, LGBTQ, and children’s experiences
  • Aged care crisis expected to peak by end of July as minister unveils new measures
  • Albanese calls emergency national cabinet meeting as premiers push for pandemic leave reboot
  • Life moves pretty fast - as do interest rate increases
  • Supersized rate hike ahead after unemployment falls to shock low of 3.5 per cent
  • Pandemic spending must be wound back to fix the budget
  • Cartoons of the week

  • Hit twice by a lightning strike
  • Stuart MacGill denies stalking Sydney bar owner

  • New Zealand announces new COVID measures as BA.5 variant takes over
  • Starbucks closes 16 coffee shops due to safety fears
  • ‘This place will crack’: Centuries-old graves threatened by the sea
  • War Crimes in Ukraine - The Missing
  • Ukraine, Russia reach deal on grain, but peace ‘a long way away’
  • Netflix to introduce cheaper service with ads
  • Musk would have begged me for money, says Trump, in escalating spat
  • ‘Disgruntled’ engineer convicted over massive WikiLeaks release of CIA secrets
  • Fresh concerns over gun safety after duo pinpoint registered Perth firearm houses
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Friday, July 15
  • Man allegedly bashed and robbed in Perth
  • Wind surfer killed in WA
  • Police crush car after catching alleged serial hoon
  • Windsurfer in his 50s unable to be revived off Perth coastal suburb
  • Scientists sink teeth into dino diet research after rare fossil find
  • Promising research points way to better type 1 diabetes treatment
  • Man who accused neighbour of sexually harassing wife sued for defamation
  • Star probe to call witnesses on whether issues drifted ‘over the border’

  • Queensland State of Origin victory breaks live-streaming records
  • The Wrap: ASX rises as higher commodity prices lifts mining giants

  • ASIC action forces trading house spruiked by Steve Smith to close
  • Bill Gates gives almost $30 billion to stem ‘significant suffering’
  • Euro falls to equal US dollar for first time in 20 years
  • Australia’s biggest investors ramp up bonds in bet on RBA policy

  • Alibrandi on stage: have they ruined the book, or given it new life?

  • Sydney Opera House lifts curtain on newly renovated Concert Hall
  • Judge rejects Amber Heard's retrial bid
  • Rylance of the Slams: Greatest actor of his generation plays the worst golfer of his

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 14 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Strike kills civilians in Vinnytsia; countries pledged $20 million for ICC
  • Italy in crisis as president rejects premier Draghi’s offer to resign
  • Russian strikes in Vinnytsia kill at least 23, with gruesome civilian toll
  • Sri Lanka president finally resigns after fleeing to a second country
  • Amid Griner trial, Russia warns U.S. against pressure for prisoner swap
  • A Twitter ‘rando’ named a new coronavirus variant Centaurus and it stuck
  • Who will be the next U.K. prime minister? 5 candidates are still standing.
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Secret Van Gogh self-portrait discovered by X-ray of another painting
  • Biden defends visit to Saudi Arabia despite Khashoggi killing
  • Sri Lankans rose up as inflation soared: A visual timeline of the crisis
  • Polish abortion activist’s trial hints at a post-Roe future in U.S.
  • Ukraine says Big Tech has dropped the ball on Russian propaganda
  • Turkey talks on grain blockade were a ‘critical step,’ U.N. chief says
  • Witnesses for Griner to testify at her drug trial in Moscow
  • China set to reveal ‘very bad’ economic picture after covid lockdowns
  • Biden walks in Trump’s footsteps in the Middle East
  • House tries to avoid messy political battles over Pentagon policy bill
  • UNESCO: Nearly half of Telegram’s Holocaust content contains denial, distortion
  • Chaos in Sri Lanka after protesters seize prime minister’s office
  • IMF reaches preliminary deal with Pakistan on $6B bailout
  • Honduras ex-President Lobo's son killed in attack
  • Pakistani officials: Baluch insurgents abduct, kill officer
  • Mali suspends rotation flights of UN peacekeepers
  • Bodies of migrants who died in Texas trailer come to Mexico
  • Tops market to reopen in Buffalo, two months after racist massacre
  • Alex Murdaugh, South Carolina attorney, charged with murdering wife and son
  • Woman says Walmart fired her for ‘problematic’ breast milk pumping
  • Texas sues Biden administration for requiring abortions in medical emergencies
  • All residents accounted for after southwest Virginia flooding
  • A post-Roe surge could reshape this Illinois steel mill town
  • New York bodega worker’s murder charge sparks self-defense debate
  • Three people not yet located after flooding in rural southwest Va., police say
  • A CEO stood trial twice in Calif. slaying. He’s now charged again.
  • A small bookstore was scammed. The local community stepped in to save it.
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says U.S. is ‘not going to wait forever’ for Iran to rejoin nuclear deal
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says inflation ‘unacceptably high’ but not as bad as it looks
  • Fed inspector general probe clears Powell, Clarida of violating laws
  • Surfside tragedy makes condo buying challenging nationwide
  • Dow tumbles 400 points as inflation, rate hikes stoke recession fears
  • Calculate how much more mortgages will cost as interest rates rise
  • Mortgage rates spike as concerns about inflation persist
  • In Hollywood, a crypto platform that isn’t just a way to lose your savings
  • I know I need to go on disability leave, but I’m still terrified
  • Fertilizer crisis delivers profits and pain as Ukraine fallout broadens
  • Bill Gates gives foundation $20 billion to fight ‘huge global setbacks’
  • Radio stations don’t have to check if programmers are foreign agents
  • Sony announces PlayStation Stars, a loyalty program for gamers
  • Google’s campus is trying the hardest, but can perks compete with WFH?
  • A Chevy Chase family refreshes a stately Colonial
  • Former medical clinic space converted to housing on Capitol Hill
  • Military Mission Creep Threatens Brazil’s Democracy
  • JPMorgan’s Bad Earnings News Really Isn’t So Bad
  • Are We in a Recession Now? Just Look at Discount Retailers
  • Pass Rights Laws Now, Democrats. Messages Can Wait.
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means: QuickTake
  • As stars and politicians age, we’re entering a new era of stagnation
  • Why building abortion clinics on federal land in red states is a bad idea
  • Harry Truman bucked inflation. Both parties can learn from him.
  • Your questions about covid-19, answered by Dr. Leana Wen
  • James Webb Space Telescope images ranked by how good they look to eat
  • Why are Trump and Musk feuding? They’re the same.
  • Sorry, protesters. Picketing a restaurant won’t stop gentrification.
  • Enforce the anti-picketing law in Montgomery County
  • A deportation policy in chaos — thanks to one judge
  • There’s still plenty to learn about Jan. 6. More witnesses must talk.
  • Yes, Fox News: This is the post-Roe world you wanted!
  • Why the right called the 10-year-old rape victim story a hoax
  • Here’s how to make red-flag laws most effective against gun violence
  • Biden’s Saudi dance
  • Cartoon by Pedro X. Molina
  • How Supreme Court reform unites Canadian conservatives and American liberals
  • Climate change is a bigger threat to Pakistan than terrorism
  • The question of compromise on abortion
  • McConnell’s machinations
  • DAR dishonors my mother
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
  • Send a letter to the editor
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 14 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The Open 2022: first round at St Andrews – live!
  • France v Belgium: Women’s Euro 2022, Group D – live!
  • England beat India by 100 runs in second ODI to level the series – live!

  • Jurowski Conducts Stravinsky Vol 1 review | Andrew Clements's classical album of the week
  • ‘My voice lends itself to sadness​ –​ I carry a lot of grief’​: Rebekah Del Rio, David Lynch’s musical muse​
  • Mark Haddon pledges all future US royalties to abortion rights groups
  • ‘Two out of five stories should be hot’: why pre-code cinema was a golden age for women
  • High-profile drubbings are rare but they happen – in men’s football too | Anita Asante
  • Prince Andrew’s Newsnight interview to be the subject of a new film
  • Russian war slowing growth and hiking inflation, European Commission warns
  • Kevin Hart: Will Smith is ‘in a better space’ after Oscars slap
  • Dining across the divide: ‘There’s a sea of white, working-class people who feel totally alienated’
  • Alleged book thief Filippo Bernardini may avoid trial in the US
  • Revealed: Queen’s sweeping immunity from more than 160 laws
  • Tom Courtenay: ‘I told Daniel Craig I turned down Bond because of the gym work’
  • The struggle to get a good holiday meal is real. Plan ahead | Jay Rayner
  • Chelsea decide against signing Cristiano Ronaldo after talks with representatives
  • Who should succeed Boris Johnson as Tory leader? Our panel’s verdict

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 14 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 14 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-14 17:53PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong researchers create algorithm to predict effectiveness of vaccines against variants with 95 per cent accuracy
  • Wharf Reic warns of interim loss as value of investment properties erodes amid poor business, economic conditions
  • Hong Kong’s Democratic Party cancels banquet amid Covid-19 concerns and criticism in pro-Beijing newspapers
  • Hong Kong housing shortage: government acts on John Lee pledge to ‘spare no effort’, sets stage for tenfold increase in homes to be built in second quarter
  • Hong Kong protests: 6 Hong Kong fugitives in failed escape bid to Taiwan admit trying to hinder criminal proceedings
  • 1.16 tonnes of drugs seized at Hong Kong airport in first half of 2022, double that of same period in 2021
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong technology minister dismisses need for real-name registration on ‘Leave Home Safe’ app; 3,674 new Covid cases logged
  • Hong Kong protests: veteran activist ‘Grandma Wong’ jailed for 32 weeks for role in 2 unauthorised assemblies
  • Poor, digitally illiterate elderly Hongkongers to face difficulties using new health code system for identifying coronavirus cases: concern groups
  • Hong Kong props up city’s weakening dollar as capital heads for the exit on possible full-point rate hike to tame record US inflation
  • An An, world’s oldest male giant panda in captivity at Hong Kong’s Ocean Park, ‘has lost appetite and is in low spirits’
  • Hunt for ‘gang of con artists’ suspected of duping 4 wanted Hong Kong protesters out of HK$400,000 before tipping off police on whereabouts
  • Hong Kong health minister Lo Chung-mau is in the hot seat - and his public appearances reveal how he plans to steer city out of pandemic, and more
  • Cryptocurrency start-ups fear new Hong Kong licensing regime falls short of linking them to main street finance
  • Ease Hong Kong’s brain drain by re-educating and integrating elderly into workforce
  • Study in China finds healthy thin people eat and exercise less rather than burning off excess calories
  • China facing more economic woes ahead of GDP release with mortgage boycott in over 80 cities
  • Weibo vows to regulate homonyms, ‘misspelt’ words if they are used to evade China’s strict censorship
  • China’s powerful sun-gazing telescope ready for launch
  • On Russian oil, China shuns US call to cap prices and urges dialogue in ‘very complicated’ issue
  • Covid-19: Beijing compound staff meet backlash after demanding quarantined residents wear wristband monitor
  • Shanghai game company hit with 1.1 million yuan fine for publishing unlicensed titles
  • China heatwave brings record high temperatures to Shanghai and other cities
  • Chinese province of Hebei detains hundreds in anti-crime blitz prompted by attack on women in restaurant
  • Hong Kong banks face higher risks on mainland lending as China’s economy slows, property woes deepen, Moody’s says
  • Chinese premier says discrimination against job applicants who have had Covid will be punished
  • Chinese word processor WPS accused of censorship after author says she was locked out of 1.3 million-character document
  • Vietnam agrees on fishing hotline with China but pace of trade ‘slow’
  • China housing analysts call for more stimulus as July sales plunge and report predicts 17 per cent slump through 2025
  • US-China accounting war: SEC chief casts doubt that a deal can avert delisting of New York-listed Chinese stocks
  • Climate change: S&P says shortfall ahead for copper and could derail decarbonisation globally, unless production is expanded hugely
  • Oil price rise offers China chance to upgrade new energy strategy as petrol price squeezes economy
  • Australia’s Albanese ‘very confident’ China military base won’t be built in Solomon Islands
  • China’s Big Tech companies, from Tencent to ByteDance, cut back on strategic investments as Beijing’s scrutiny continues
  • Chinese city’s health system buckles under Covid debt strain
  • Swiss army knife maker Victorinox targets China’s millennials and Gen Z with digital push, personalised products
  • Global crises pushing US and Europe into closer commercial partnership, top EU trade official says
  • China heatwave prompts energy curbs for manufacturing hub, but repeat of power crisis unlikely
  • China’s Henan bank scandal not just a financial crisis, it may be a lasting political calamity: analysts
  • US House bill aims to press China to restart Tibet talks with Dalai Lama
  • Britain and Japan set to merge military fighter programmes costing ‘tens of billions of dollars’, sources say
  • Japan PM Kishida blames police for death of former leader Shinzo Abe
  • UAE to invest US$2 billion in India for ‘food parks’ amid growing global food insecurity
  • Did Nepal’s China-India balancing act cause it to ditch the US-run SPP security initiative?
  • Coronavirus: Fresh Covid-19 wave sweeps Asia, NZ warns of pressure on hospitals
  • Smile or be fired, Philippine mayor tells employees
  • Sri Lanka crisis: President Gotabaya Rajapaksa emails resignation after fleeing to Singapore
  • Ukraine severs ties with North Korea after pro-Russia separatists recognition
  • Singapore central bank tightens monetary policy in surprise move to cool inflation
  • Why South Korea’s Catholic bishops want dormant death penalty to be fully abolished
  • Italian PM Mario Draghi offers to quit, but president rejects his resignation
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky urges ‘special tribunal’ into Russian invasion following latest deadly attack
  • ‘No nuclear Iran’ Israeli PM vows after Biden talk to strengthen alliance
  • Kevin Spacey pleads not guilty to sex offences, faces trial next year
  • Ukraine lobbying US for longer-range missiles after ‘HIMARS strike kills Russian general’
  • PM4PM? Penny Mordaunt rises through ranks to vie for UK leadership
  • Rape of 10-year-old jolts US abortion debate
  • Britain’s Covid-19 death toll passes 200,000 as Omicron subvariants drive new wave of cases
  • Nuclear attack survival ad alarms New Yorkers
  • Russia’s war in Ukraine is more than just a battle of ‘good vs evil’
  • Bill Gates donates US$20 billion to his foundation, says he plans to ‘give away all my wealth’ in future
  • Nato chief, challenged on the need to confront China, cites its military build-up and rights record
  • Amber Heard’s bid for new trial with Johnny Depp rejected by US judge
  • After Israel fist bump fiasco, can Joe Biden avoid handshake with Saudi crown prince?
  • UN chief sees ‘ray of hope’ after breakthrough at Ukraine-Russia grain talks in Türkiye
  • Ending Russia’s grain blockade won’t solve global food crisis
  • KKR to buy 25 per cent stake in Li Ka-shing-backed British water company for US$1.02 billion
  • TSMC posts robust quarterly earnings but sees chip inventory building up at some customers amid supply chain adjustment
  • Will China’s ‘zero-Covid’ policy thwart its plan for infrastructure-fuelled stimulus?
  • Hong Kong stocks slip as traders dump Chinese developers amid growing distress while Alibaba, Tencent sustain tech advance
  • Netflix movie review: The Gray Man – Chris Evans, Ryan Gosling in entertaining action thriller from Avengers: Endgame directors’
  • 5 mindful ways to practise self-care and relieve stress this summer if you’re stuck in Hong Kong
  • How to fight climate change: re-wear your clothes – if we all used them for twice as long as now, emissions from clothing would fall 44 per cent
  • How the words ‘monsoon’ and ‘trade wind’ blew into the English language
  • K-drama star Seo In-guk on new single album Love&Love and why he enjoys his Cafe Minamdang role so much
  • People with disabilities gain from internships in kitchen, restaurants at five-star hotel in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong Film Awards at a crossroads: Derek Yee, HKFA chairman, on Hong Kong cinema’s awkward future
  • Hong Kong’s new dinosaur exhibition warns of human extinction at the hands of climate change and the perils that face our environment
  • Who are Elvis Presley’s grandchildren, and where are they now? Lisa Marie, his only child, had 4 kids – actress Riley Keough, the late Benjamin Keough and twins Finley and Harper Lockwood
  • Meet Mathilde Pinault, heiress to the Kering fashion empire: the equestrian daughter of billionaire François-Henri Pinault admires Hollywood stepmum Salma Hayek, and is a Paris Fashion Week regular
  • Dumped, sued and exposed: is this Elon Musk’s worst week yet? From that Twitter lawsuit and his secret twins revelation with Shivon Zilis, to girlfriend Natasha Bassett breaking up with him
  • Wimbledon wives! Jelena Djokovic and Mery Perelló’s fashion, compared: Novak Djokovic and Rafael Nadal may be tennis greats, but their partners are style icons decked in Chanel, Cartier and Balenciaga
  • 5 designers giving traditional Asian fashions a modern update: Star Wars’ Kelly Marie Tran wore an ao dai by Thai Nguyen to the Oscars 2022, while Rihanna donned a Guo Pei look at the Met Gala
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
